summary: Rapid Spin is the safest broad-value line because it removes Stealth Rock, improves speed, and still pressures a weakened Dragapult that is already burned.

rankedActions.label: Rapid Spin
rankedActions.rationale: It improves board state immediately, keeps Great Tusk healthy for later, and is less punishable than a raw Ground move into possible immunities or pivots.
assumptions: Dragapult is unlikely to stay in repeatedly at low health.
Hazard removal has ongoing value for the rest of the game.
risks: If Dragapult stays and attacks, Great Tusk still takes chip.
This may miss a direct KO if a stronger attack was safe.

rankedActions.label: Knock Off
rankedActions.rationale: Knock Off is flexible and punishes a switch while still threatening Dragapult.
assumptions: Opponent may pivot out of Dragapult.
risks: May be weaker than the immediate best damage line.

rankedActions.label: Ice Spinner
rankedActions.rationale: Direct pressure into Dragapult can be attractive if you mainly want to threaten the current active slot.
assumptions: You value immediate tempo over hazard control.
risks: Loses value if the opponent switches to a sturdier resist or check.

assumptions: No unrevealed immunity pivot makes Headlong Rush clearly best.
You prefer a stable second-opinion line over an all-in punish.
dangerFlags: Do not blindly click Headlong Rush if a Ghost or Levitate pivot is still plausible.
If Great Tusk is a key win condition, avoid unnecessary chip.
